events.title: Road to 250 Season Opening
events.description: Rocky Mount's America 250 year begins. First 250 community enrollment opens—be among the first Tennesseans to join the celebration.

events.title: Spring Break Camp Week 1
events.description: Spring break adventure at Rocky Mount for Johnson City & Bristol school students. Hands-on frontier activities, crafts, and historical exploration.

events.title: Spring Break Camp Week 2
events.description: Spring break adventure at Rocky Mount for Sullivan County school students. Hands-on frontier activities, crafts, and historical exploration.

events.title: James P. Byrd Guest Lecture: Colonial Religion
events.speaker: Dr. James P. Byrd
events.description: Explore how faith shaped life on the Tennessee frontier. Dr. Byrd examines the role of religion in colonial America and its influence on early settlers.

events.title: Sullivan County 250
events.description: Celebrate Sullivan County's role in America's founding. Special programming highlighting local Revolutionary War history and the county's contribution to the Overmountain Men.

events.title: Woolly Days & Colonial Gardening
events.description: Experience sheep shearing, wool processing, and colonial gardening techniques. This year featuring special America 250 interpretation connecting frontier agriculture to the Revolutionary era.

events.title: Homeschool Day - Spring
events.description: Special programming for homeschool families. Living history tour, hands-on crafts, and educational activities designed for home educators.

events.title: Early Frontier Days & Colonial Trade Fair
events.description: Three days celebrating frontier life and Revolutionary-era trade. Living history demonstrations, period vendors, militia encampments, and authentic colonial crafts. In collaboration with the Overmountain Victory Trail Association.

events.title: Tennessee's 230th Birthday
events.description: On June 1, 1796, Tennessee became the 16th state. Today we celebrate 230 years of statehood. This is also the FINAL DAY to enroll in the First 250 program.

events.title: America 250 Flag Weekend
events.description: TN 230 Recognition, America 250 Flag Dedication ceremony, and the Betsy Ross Sip & Sew social event. Celebrate Tennessee's 230th birthday with flag history and textile arts.

events.title: Summer Camp Week 1
events.description: Week-long summer camp adventure at Rocky Mount. Leadership, nature, and history for young explorers. Hands-on activities, frontier skills, and historical discovery.

events.title: Father's Day Weekend at the Forge
events.description: Parent and child blacksmithing experience. Work side-by-side at the forge to create a keepsake. Limited to 10 pairs—reserve early.

events.title: Summer Camp Week 2
events.description: Week-long summer camp adventure at Rocky Mount. Leadership, nature, and history for young explorers. Hands-on activities, frontier skills, and historical discovery.

events.title: Colonial Independence Day
events.description: America's 250th Birthday at the place where Tennessee's government began. The signature event of our commemorative year featuring the First 250 ceremony—where enrolled participants' names will be read aloud—period demonstrations, and patriotic celebration.

events.title: Back to School at the Frontier
events.description: Last adventure before school starts! Family-friendly frontier fun with games, crafts, and activities for all ages.

events.title: Homeschool Day - Late Summer
events.description: Special programming for homeschool families. Living history tour, hands-on crafts, and educational activities designed for home educators.

events.title: First Families of Tennessee Reunion
events.description: A first-ever gathering of descendants of Tennessee's founding families. Friday events for registered descendants only; Saturday and Sunday open to the public. Oral history recordings, genealogy resources, and celebration of Tennessee's pioneer heritage.

events.title: OVTA Victory Day
events.description: Commemorate the Overmountain Men's march to Kings Mountain. In partnership with the Overmountain Victory Trail Association. Date pending OVTA confirmation.

events.title: Harvest Fest
events.description: Celebrate the autumn harvest with demonstrations of colonial food preservation, apple butter making, and period crafts. This year featuring special America 250 interpretation.

events.title: Haunting on the Mount
events.description: Experience Rocky Mount after dark. Spooky tales from Tennessee's frontier past, candlelit tours, and seasonal treats. Thursday through Saturday evenings (Oct 15-17 & 22-24).

events.title: Frontier Christmas
events.description: Discover how frontier families celebrated the holidays. Period decorations, traditional crafts, and warm hospitality. Wednesday through Saturday (Nov 4-7, 11-14, 18-21).

events.title: Candlelight Christmas
events.description: Rocky Mount by candlelight. Experience the magic of a colonial Christmas with carolers, warm cider, and the glow of candles throughout the historic site. Friday and Saturday evenings (Dec 4-5 & 11-12).

blacksmithWorkshops.title: Blacksmith Workshops
blacksmithWorkshops.description: Learn the art of the forge with hands-on blacksmithing instruction.

hearthCooking.title: Hearth Cooking
hearthCooking.description: Experience authentic 18th-century cooking techniques at the open hearth.

twilightOnTheMount.title: Twilight on the Mount
twilightOnTheMount.description: Evening tours of Rocky Mount as the sun sets. A unique perspective on frontier life.

homeschoolDays.title: Homeschool Days
homeschoolDays.description: Special programming for homeschool families with living history tours and hands-on activities.

behindTheScenes.title: Behind the Scenes Exclusive Tour
behindTheScenes.description: Limited-capacity tour with artifact handling, collections access, heritage animal encounters, and coffee & pastries. Last Saturday of each month.
